zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Brionne-Bienfaite de Clare於1035年11月5日出生在法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯沙吉馬-德-比法特-拉-可雷頌尼艾時，他的父親Gilbert Crispin de Brionne16歲，母親Adelise de Clermont16歲。他於1054年與Rohese Giffard de Bolbec在法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯隆維爾結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1089年5月在英國英格蘭素福爾可克萊爾享年53歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾聖 辛茲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Brionne-Bienfaite de Clare was born on 5 November 1035, in Saint-Martin-de-Bienfaite-la-Cressonnière, Calvados, Normandy, France, his father, Gilbert Crispin de Brionne, was 16 and his mother, Adelise de Clermont, was 16. He married Rohese Giffard de Bolbec in 1054, in Longueville, Calvados, Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. He died about May 1089, in Clare, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53, and was buried in St Neots, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Brionne-Bienfaite de Clare est né le 5 novembre 1035, à Saint-Martin-de-Bienfaite-la-Cressonnière, Calvados, Normandie, France, son père, Gilbert Crispin de Brionne, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Adelise de Clermont, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Rohese Giffard de Bolbec en 1054, à Longueville, Calvados, Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. Il est décédé environ mai 1089, à Clare, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à St Neots, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat 1054 in Longueville, Calvados, Normandie, Frankreich Rohese Giffard de Bolbec geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Töchter. Er starb um Mai 1089 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Clare, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in St Neots, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Brionne-Bienfaite de Clare(이)가 1035년 11월 5일 프랑스 노먼디 캘바도스 세인트-마틴-데-비파이테-라-크레손이e르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert Crispin de Brionne(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelise de Clermont(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1054년 프랑스 노먼디 캘바도스 론구에빌에서 Rohese Giffard de Bolbec(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1089년 5월 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 클래르에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 스트 니오츠에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Brionne-Bienfaite de Clare родился 5 ноября 1035 г. в местности сеинт-мартин-де-биенфеите-ла-крессонниeр, калвадос, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Gilbert Crispin de Brionne было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelise de Clermont, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Rohese Giffard de Bolbec в 1054 г. в местности лонгуевилл, калвадос, норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Он умер около май 1089 г. в местности клар, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности ст неоц, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания.
